value.description: Corvega factory systems for automating extraction, transfer, and fabrication across a proper pre-War works floor.
Construct basic Corvega works, including conveyors, underground transfer points, and factory hoppers for automating routine industrial tasks around the colony.
Construct specialized Corvega factory systems capable of manufacturing components, producing alloy stock, and handling other advanced automated production jobs.
A simple Corvega conveyor belt capable of moving an item from one place to another. Due to safety regulations, items on the belt cannot be interacted with while in transit.
A Corvega factory hopper for holding feedstock and finished output for attached works machinery.

A large Corvega automated field machine that combines sowing and harvesting across a designated outdoor zone. It deposits harvested goods into attached factory hoppers or drops them on the ground if hoppers are not present, and can be set to cycle automatically once most plants in the zone are fully grown.

An automated Corvega deep-crust drilling rig for extracting stone and buried metals from under the surface.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption.

A Corvega automated smelter that extracts usable metal from slag chunks, broken-down weapons, apparel, stone chunks, and mechanoid corpses using strong electromagnets and industrial heat.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

A Corvega automated refinery that converts biological matter such as wood and plant stock into chemfuel.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

An industrial Corvega masonry saw capable of cutting stone chunks into usable blocks.
Factory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
A large Corvega field machine that handles sowing and harvest cycles across a designated crop zone, then pushes the yield into attached works hoppers.

An automated Corvega refinery that converts wood and other organic feedstock into oil. It requires a factory floor, works hoppers, and can be overclocked for higher output at higher heat and power cost.

An automated Corvega deep extraction rig for pulling stone and buried metals up from the crust. Works machinery needs hoppers for intake and output, throws off serious heat, and can be overclocked at the cost of even greater draw and temperature.
A Corvega masonry line for turning stone chunks into consistent construction blocks. It needs hoppers, throws heat, and responds well to overclocking if your grid can support it.
A heavy Corvega smelter for recovering metal from slag, scrap gear, stone, and mechanoid remains. It belongs on a factory floor and can be overclocked for harsher but faster reclamation.
A basic industrial conveyor for moving materials between work cells with minimal labor handling.
Stores feedstock and finished goods for automated Corvega works machinery.
A buried intake point that lets an industrial conveyor run beneath short stretches of ground before resurfacing.
A buried discharge point that brings an underground conveyor back to the surface near the next work cell.
